title: Preference pivots update the existing fact instead of duplicating it
description: When a stored preference is superseded (npm -> pnpm), a well-behaved agent updates the existing fact. If a naive add leaves both facts, curation dry-run must propose deleting the older superseded preference.
contract_notes: Write-time add remains report-only for supersession, but deterministic curation dry-run enforces hygiene by proposing deletion of the older superseded fact.

facts.content: User prefers npm as the package manager for this project.

args.content: User prefers pnpm as the package manager for this project (supersedes earlier npm preference).
User prefers pnpm as the package manager for this project (supersedes earlier npm preference).

prompts: Use your memory tools to update existing project memory. The durable preference is now pnpm, superseding the earlier npm preference. Update or replace the existing entry instead of adding a duplicate. Reply done.

assertions.name: single_package_manager_fact
assertions.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM memory_facts WHERE content LIKE '%package manager%'

assertions.name: pivot_to_pnpm_present
assertions.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM memory_facts WHERE content LIKE '%pnpm%'

assertions.name: stale_npm_preference_gone
assertions.sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM memory_facts WHERE content LIKE '%prefers npm%'
